title: MATRIX Procurement Domain Extension v0.2
description: Domain extension for the MATRIX Core Event Schema v0.2 supporting AI-assisted public-sector procurement review. Activated when the core field domain_extension equals procurement. Designed to preserve procurement integrity, institutional accountability, traceability, auditability, contestability, fail-closed governance, and public trust. Privacy-by-design: no raw personal data, no confidential supplier secrets, no discriminatory supplier scoring, and no opaque exclusion without human review.

procurement_case_id.description: Unique non-personal identifier of the procurement review case under MATRIX governance. Must not encode personal or supplier-confidential data.

tender_reference.description: Pseudonymous, public, or institutional reference identifier for the tender or call for proposals. Must not contain confidential commercial content.

supplier_reference.description: Pseudonymous reference identifier of the candidate supplier or economic operator. Must not include personal data of natural persons or trade secrets. Use NOT_APPLICABLE when the procurement stage does not involve a specific supplier.

procurement_stage.description: Stage of the public procurement lifecycle in which the AI-supported review event takes place.

risk_indicator.description: Structured non-discriminatory risk indicators produced by the AI-supported review. Indicators must be explainable and reviewable by humans; raw scores alone must not justify exclusion.

risk_level.description: Aggregated risk level associated with the tender or supplier file under review.

items.description: Controlled category of procurement risk identified during AI-supported review.

risk_categories.description: List of identified procurement risk categories, drawn from a controlled vocabulary.

explanation_reference.description: Reference, URI, or document ID to a human-readable explanation of how the risk indicator was produced, supporting transparency and contestability.

compliance_score.description: Structured compliance assessment of the supplier or tender against legal and regulatory procurement requirements. Must not be used as the sole basis for exclusion without human review.

score_value.description: Normalized compliance score in the range 0 to 1, where 1 indicates full assessed compliance with applicable rules.

score_scale.description: Scale used for the compliance score. Restricted to a normalized 0 to 1 scale to prevent opaque or discriminatory scoring.

items.description: Reference to applicable legal acts, directives, regulations, procurement rules, or institutional procedures.

legal_basis_reference.description: Legal bases against which compliance is evaluated.

conflict_of_interest_flag.description: Indicator of potential or confirmed conflicts of interest detected during procurement review. Personal data of natural persons must not be embedded; only pseudonymous role references are permitted.

status.description: Status of conflict-of-interest detection for this procurement event.

role_reference.description: Pseudonymous reference to the institutional role involved, without identifying natural persons.

mitigation_measure.description: Description or reference to mitigation measures applied, such as recusal, reassignment, or additional oversight.

procurement_integrity_status.description: Overall procurement integrity status for the event, reflecting institutional assessment of fairness, transparency, and lawfulness.

human_procurement_validation.description: Mandatory human-in-the-loop validation record. Public authority decisions must not be fully automated; a qualified human reviewer must validate AI-supported outputs.

validation_status.description: Outcome of the human review of the AI-supported procurement assessment.

validator_role.description: Institutional role of the human validator. Names of natural persons must not be stored here.

validation_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the human validation event.

validation_notes_reference.description: Reference, URI, or document ID to the structured human validation notes stored in the institutional record system.

oversight_body_status.description: Status of independent oversight body involvement, such as a procurement review authority, supreme audit institution, anti-corruption authority, or internal oversight function.

status.description: State of oversight body engagement for this procurement event.

oversight_body_reference.description: Institutional reference of the oversight body, such as legal name, internal registry identifier, or oversight function reference.

decision_reference.description: Reference to the formal oversight decision document, if issued.

appeal_or_contestation_status.description: Record of contestability mechanisms exercised by suppliers or other legitimate parties, ensuring the right to appeal or contest AI-supported procurement decisions.

status.description: Current status of any appeal or contestation related to this procurement event.

appeal_reference.description: Pseudonymous reference identifier of the appeal or contestation case.

remedy_applied.description: Type of remedy applied as a result of the appeal, if any.

audit_trail_reference.description: Reference or URI to the immutable tamper-evident audit trail entry associated with this procurement event, stored in the MATRIX institutional traceability layer.

fail_closed_procurement_reason.description: If the system halts, blocks, or refuses to issue an AI-supported procurement output, this field records the fail-closed governance rationale. Required when the core event indicates a fail-closed state.

triggered.description: Indicates whether the fail-closed mechanism was triggered for this event.

reason_code.description: Controlled reason for invoking fail-closed behavior.

reason_description_reference.description: Reference, URI, or document ID to a structured human-readable explanation of the fail-closed decision.

procurement_decision_effect.description: Effect of the AI-supported procurement event on the procurement procedure. Exclusionary or adverse effects must always be subject to human review and contestability; AI must never autonomously exclude a supplier.
